title: Zapoteca filipes
taxonomy: Zapoteca filipes is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a member of the genus Zapoteca. Zapoteca filipes is native to Central America. This species is part of the legume family, which includes a wide variety of plants. Zapoteca filipes is further classified as a member of the subfamily Faboideae.
uses: Zapoteca filipes is a plant species that has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its medicinal uses are largely based on its roots and leaves. The roots of Zapoteca filipes are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. Additionally, the plant is believed to possess antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating infections and wounds. Its leaves are also used to treat fever and other respiratory issues. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and potential side effects of using Zapoteca filipes for medicinal purposes.
constituents: Zapoteca filipes contains triterpenoids and flavonoids as active constituents. These compounds are known to have potential medicinal properties, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Research on the plant has shown that its extracts exhibit inhibitory effects on various enzymes and pathogens. The triterpenoids present in Zapoteca filipes have been found to have immunomodulatory and anti-proliferative effects. Further studies are needed to fully understand the pharmacological properties and potential applications of Zapoteca filipes.

intro: Zapoteca filipes, also known as Guanacaste or Mexican Buckeye, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central America, characterized by its vibrant yellow flowers and unique seed pods. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Zapoteca filipes include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory infections and other health conditions.
